rulings.Case Title: DONALD FULTON et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 1, 2008, line 3. DEFENDANT PNEUMO ABEX, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied for failure to shift the burden of proof. Defendant's reliance on plaintiff's objections to special interrogatories does not shift the burden of producing evidence and the Kentfield Declaration lacks the foundational facts to do so. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES LOUIE et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 1, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANT CONOCOPHILLIPS COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifts the burden of producing evidence based on plaintiff's factually devoid discovery. The declarations of Ay and Ganzhorn are not admissible to establish exposure. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY J JACQUES VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, DBA SAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 1, 2008, line 20. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied for failure to shift the burden of producing evidence. Defendant has failed to demonstrate a lack of evidence of knowledge of the potential assault before it occurred and as a consequence, does not negate the claim of duty. On the issue of breach of duty, the Hui declaration in the moving papers is ambiguous as to when the call was made to central control. The Browne Declaration also creates a triable issue of fact. On the issue of causation, the Browne Declaration creates a triable issue of fact. The Browne Declaration is admissible to address a common carrier's duty. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's evidence are overruled. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: SWISSPORT NORTH AMERICA, INC., A DELAWARE VS. JOHN MUIR HEALTH, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Compliance With Arbitration Order;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 1, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT JOHN MUIR HEALTH'S Motion For Order Compelling Compliance With Arbitration Order is granted. Defendant has demonstrated that plaintiff has knowingly sought out and participated in (through Benesight) the benefits of the contract containing the arbitration clause. For that reason, the arbitration provision may be enforced against a non-signatory. Comer v. Micor, Inc. (2005) 436 F. 3d 1098. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD DONG et al VS. HARRY WOO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 1, 2008, line 19. PLAINTIFFS ROLAND DONG, JEANETTE CORDERO, ANGELA POLAND AND DESIREE POLAND'S DEMURRER is overruled; the moving parties are to answer within 10 days. The decision in Smith v. Exxon Mobil Oil Corp. (2007) 153 Cal. App. 4th 1407, is the basis for the Court's decision. The record does not support the conclusion that Woo had a full and fail opportunity to litigate the issues presented by the cross-complaint. He did not appear because of a misunderstanding on the part of his counsel; discovery was not available in the prior proceeding; Woo has presented evidence that raises a question as to Dong's status; and the cross-complaint includes parties who were involved in the Rent Board proceeding. =(302/PJM/PB)
